question: Synonym of WHIRL
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Reap : cut or gather (a crop or harvest). Turn : move in a circular direction wholly or partly round an axis or point. Agitate : make (someone) troubled or nervous. Weep : shed tears. Whirl : move or cause to move rapidly round and round. Synonym of Whirl is Turn

question: Synonym of RAKISH
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Frivolous : not having any serious purpose or value. Dashing : (of a man) attractive, adventurous, and full of confidence. Aggressive : ready or likely to attack or confront; characterized by or resulting from aggression. Childish : of, like, or appropriate to a child. Rakish : having or displaying a dashing, jaunty, or slightly disreputable quality or appearance. Synonym of Rakish is Dashing

question: Synonym of BRASH
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Slow : moving or operating, or designed to do so, only at a low speed; not quick or fast. Cultured : characterized by refined taste and manners and good education. Aggressive : ready or likely to attack or confront; characterized by or resulting from aggression. Humble : having or showing a modest or low estimate of one's importance. Brash : self-assertive in a rude, noisy, or overbearing way. Synonym of Brash is Aggressive

question: Synonym of AMICABLE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Interesting : arousing curiosity or interest; holding or catching the attention. Loving : feeling or showing love or great care. Affectionate : readily feeling or showing fondness or tenderness. Friendly : kind and pleasant. Amicable : characterized by friendliness and absence of discord. Synonym of Amicable is Friendly

question: Synonym of ACCOLADE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Welcome : an instance or manner of greeting someone. Award : give or order the giving of (something) as an official payment, compensation, or prize to (someone). Affection : a gentle feeling of fondness or liking. Arrival : the action or process of arriving. Accolade : an award or privilege granted as a special honour or as an acknowledgement of merit. Synonym of Accolade is Award

question: Synonym of IMPREGNATE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Conceal : not allow to be seen; hide. Suffer : experience or be subjected to (something bad or unpleasant). Infuse : fill; pervade. Conclude : bring or come to an end. Impregnate : soak or saturate (something) with a substance. Synonym of Impregnate is Infuse

question: Synonym of RAP
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Transfer : move from one place to another. Criticise : indicate the faults of (someone or something) in a disapproving way. Advise : offer suggestions about the best course of action to someone. Participate : be involved; take part. Rap : criticize severely. Synonym of Rap is Criticise

question: Synonym of CAVEAT
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Dispute : a disagreement or argument. Challenge : a call to someone to participate in a competitive situation or fight to decide who is superior in terms of ability or strength. Advice : guidance or recommendations offered with regard to prudent future action. Warning : a statement or event that warns of something or that serves as a cautionary example. Caveat : a warning or proviso of specific stipulations, conditions, or limitations. Synonym of Caveat is Warning

question: Synonym of MIRTH
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Sorrow : a feeling of deep distress caused by loss, disappointment, or other misfortune suffered by oneself or others. Enthusiasm : intense and eager enjoyment, interest, or approval. Adventure : an unusual and exciting or daring experience. Merriment : gaiety and fun. Mirth : amusement, especially as expressed in laughter. Synonym of Mirth is Merriment

question: Synonym of PROSCRIBE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Recommend : put forward (someone or something) with approval as being suitable for a particular purpose or role. Allow : let (someone) have or do something. Advance : move forwards in a purposeful way. Banish : send (someone) away from a country or place as an official punishment. Proscribe : forbid, especially by law. Synonym of Proscribe is Banish

question: Synonym of MYSTIQUE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Fame : the state of being known or talked about by many people, especially on account of notable achievements. Reputation : the beliefs or opinions that are generally held about someone or something. Admirable : arousing or deserving respect and approval. Popularity : the state or condition of being liked, admired, or supported by many people. Mystique : a quality of mystery, glamour, or power associated with someone or something. Synonym of Mystique is Admirable

question: Synonym of DAUNT
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Detain : keep (someone) from proceeding by holding them back or making claims on their attention. Annoy : make (someone) a little angry; irritate. Abuse : use (something) to bad effect or for a bad purpose; misuse. Intimidate : frighten or overawe (someone), especially in order to make them do what one wants. Daunt : make (someone) feel intimidated or apprehensive. Synonym of Daunt is Intimidate

question: Synonym of COMPETENCE
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Efficiency : the state or quality of being efficient. Competition : the activity or condition of striving to gain or win something by defeating or establishing superiority over others. Ability : possession of the means or skill to do something. Compensation : something, typically money, awarded to someone in recognition of loss, suffering, or injury. Competence : the ability to do something successfully or efficiently. Synonym of Competence is Ability

question: The man succumbed to his injuries the day following the day of accident.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Resist : withstand the action or effect of. Yielded : give way to arguments, demands, or pressure. Fell : cut down (a tree). Died : (of a person, animal, or plant) stop living. Succumbed : fail to resist pressure, temptation, or some other negative force. Synonym of Succumbed is Resist
